summary: Task 6 adds no new unit tests, which is consistent with the project's explicit Compose-UI-exclusion rule (patterns.md: 'What we explicitly do not test: Compose UI') and with the task's TDD Anchor. The three behavioural seams this commit touches are each already backed elsewhere: LogExportManager.buildExport(About) and writeTo(...) by LogExportManagerTest (10+ cases covering header, section order, truncation direction, fresh-install, IOException, null-stream), TapCounter by TapCounterTest (threshold, inclusive/exclusive 2s boundary, single-trigger-per-cycle, 8th-tap-no-retrigger), and CrashHandler dispatch + manifest/process-guard by CrashHandlerTest / SanctumApplicationTest. The only logic net-new in be28764 is AboutViewModel.buildAndWrite's try/IOException → Result mapping (3 lines wrapping already-tested calls), LogExportModule's two @Binds declarations (validated at runtime by the reported BUILD SUCCESSFUL), and DefaultCommandRunner.@Inject constructor() (one annotation, no behaviour change). None of these justify a new Robolectric class. The verify-user plan in tasks/6.md covers all four behavioural surfaces (US-C pipeline, US-A crash+gesture, SAF cancel, 2s-gap boundary) that instrumentation would otherwise have exercised, which is the correct split for a project without androidTest. Verdict: existing coverage is adequate and the no-new-tests decision is principled, not a gap. Existing suite reported BUILD SUCCESSFUL on this commit; no finding blocks the task.
